message:
fix(packs/docker,test): docker prune severity is High + codex warn test uses per-rule override

Two related test corrections.

src/packs/containers/docker.rs:
- Docker prune patterns (`docker system prune -a`, `docker volume
  prune`) ship at `Severity::High` in `create_destructive_patterns()`,
  but the per-pattern severity test was asserting `Severity::Critical`
  against them. Update the test to match the actual classification.
- Add the `crate::packs::Severity` import to the test module
  (rustfmt-ordered before `test_helpers::*`).
- Apply rustfmt: collapse short multi-line `assert_blocks` calls onto
  single lines.

tests/codex_hook_protocol.rs:
- The `codex_warn_path_exits_zero_with_stderr_warning` test was using
  `DCG_POLICY_DEFAULT_MODE=warn` to try to downgrade a Critical match
  (`git reset --hard`) to warn. That env-var path no longer downgrades
  Critical — only per-rule overrides in the config file can do that.
  Replace the env-only setup with a hermetic test that writes a
  config file under a per-test `HOME`:
    [policy.rules]
    "core.git:reset-hard" = "warn"
  Spawn dcg with `env_clear()` and a controlled `PATH` / `HOME` /
  `TMPDIR` / `NO_COLOR=1`, pipe the payload to stdin, and reconstruct
  the same `HookOutcome` shape the helper would have produced. The
  follow-on assertions (exit 0, stderr contains the warning) stay
  unchanged.
- Two `run_codex_hook_with_env` / `run_claude_hook_with_env` callers
  collapse to single-line argument lists per rustfmt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
